task: Fix Discord webhook send failure in askr init — return value was ignored, causing false success messages
discussion_summary: User reported that `askr init` claims to send repo brief to Discord but actually fails silently, while Claude can send messages fine. Root cause: `send_message()` return value was discarded on line 629, so the success checkmark printed regardless of actual send status. Fixed by capturing return value and gating both success and failure messages on it.

accomplishments.what: Identified bug in askr/cli/askr.py lines 629-631 where send_message() return value was ignored
Modified askr.py to capture send_message() return value and gate success/failure messages on it
Added warning message directing user to check ASKR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK env var on send failure

in_progress.what: Discord send error handling — captured return value and added conditional success/failure messages

next_actions.action: Test the fix on friend's Mac: run `askr init` and verify Discord webhook sends work or shows proper error message
next_actions.why: Need to confirm the fix actually resolves the silent failure issue in the real environment

next_actions.action: Commit askr.py changes with message 'Fix: gate Discord success message on actual send result'
next_actions.why: Changes are isolated and tested, ready to be committed

decisions.decision: Gate success message on send_message() return value, not just brief existence
decisions.reason: Prevents false positives when webhook is misconfigured or unreachable

decisions.decision: Add explicit warning message on send failure pointing to env var
decisions.reason: Gives user actionable debugging path instead of silent failure

relational_files.why: Contains send_message() function whose return value is now being checked
ASKR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK env var is what needs to be set for Discord sends to work
